channel.title: LIBRCHAIN 区块链新闻雷达
channel.description: 每小时自动更新的区块链新闻、博客与 X 热点聚合

item.title: Digital Chamber sues Illinois officials over new state 0.2% crypto tax
item.description: The group alleged that the tax signed into law in June “discriminates against people who transact in digital assets“ and should be blocked from implementation and enforcement.

item.title: Hugging Face CEO Thanks Chinese AI for Saving the Day After OpenAI Hack
item.description: When American commercial AI refused to help investigate the breach, Hugging Face ran Chinese model GLM 5.2 locally. Its CEO now says there's an important lesson in this.

item.title: New Clarity Act emerges that's a start on the final draft, makes ethics rule temporary
item.description: Lawmakers have circulated text of the latest draft that will be pushed toward a Senate finish line, but they're still debating a core government-ethics piece.

item.title: BitGo, OTC Markets plan tokenized securities access for broker-dealers
item.description: The proposed alliance would enable more than 150 broker-dealers to trade and settle digital asset securities through OTC Link ATS using BitGo’s custody infrastructure.

item.title: Bitcoin price avoids major Iran jitters as S&P 500 ‘short squeeze’ on horizon
item.description: Bitcoin and US stocks stayed strong in the face of US-Iran escalation as analysis predicted BTC price action outperforming equities later.

item.title: Revolut hits $115 billion valuation in employee share sale: WSJ
item.description: The company reported strong 2025 performance, with a $2.3 billion pre-tax profit, $6 billion in revenue, and over 75 million customers.

item.title: We're hiring a Growth Marketing Lead for Baton Corporation, the development company behind Pump fun. Pump fun grew into one of the largest crypto platforms in history with almost no paid marketing. Now we want to take it mainstream - from crypto product t - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiW0FVX3lxTFBseGM3Q2VxWTUzNUw4NE9KeVJjcDIxTTg3ZzBldXZYYzFyWDN2cGNZQ1BzMldzWFlteklLZDlDQzJ3UzdoRzVaYWhSM3N2S1dkTjltdHdIT00wVWs?oc=5" target="_blank">We're hiring a Growth Marketing Lead for Ba…

item.title: SEC's Peirce warns some DeFi vaults, onchain lending may fall under securities laws
item.description: The commissioner said onchain vaults and lending strategies could resemble investment funds or advisers, depending on how they are structured.

item.title: UK digital bond plans hinge on one missing piece: onchain cash
item.description: Experts say Britain's digital gilt pilot can unlock trapped liquidity in financial markets only if regulators approve an onchain stablecoin.

item.title: US Seizes More Than $25M in Crypto Tied to Investment and Romance Scams
item.description: Prosecutors filed five forfeiture cases as Secret Service agents traced funds from thousands of victims to launderers in Southeast Asia.

item.title: Crypto Long & Short: The crypto question isn't what to own — it's what you can survive holding
item.description: In this week's Crypto Long & Short, Lionsoul Global’s Gregory Mall writes that the primary crypto allocation decision is size, not selection.

item.title: Centralized Elements 'Frequently Persist' in DeFi and Should Be Regulated: FATF
item.description: Nearly every country has yet to apply the rules, the watchdog said, warning of outright bans for platforms that don’t comply.

item.title: U.S. seeks forfeiture of $25 million in crypto tied to romance and investment scams
item.description: The cases add to more than $800 million that authorities say a dedicated federal scam task force has recovered.

item.title: Forget Nvidia: The next big AI trade could be crypto and blockchain
item.description: Franklin Templeton’s Sandy Kaul and Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire argue that as autonomous AI agents start spending money on their own, blockchain networks will power the next big AI trade.

item.title: The digital euro: Surveillance money, or a better alternative to cash?
item.description: Proponents of the digital euro say its a better alternative to cash that’s fully backed by the ECB. But critics argue it could provide the EU with too much power over its citizens.

item.title: Franklin Templeton calls agentic AI next ‘killer’ use case for blockchain
item.description: Franklin Templeton’s digital assets lead said that the autonomous AI agent economy will increase demand for blockchain protocols hosting machine-to-machine micropayments.

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

item.title: Justin Sun's HTX 'Rotating' On-Chain Wallets Amid UK Sanctions: TRM Labs
item.description: TRM Labs says the UK-sanctioned exchange has rotated hot wallets across blockchains, leaving address-list screening struggling to keep pace.

item.title: Kalshi rolls out Midterm Hubs ahead of the November elections in the U.S.
item.description: The prediction markets platform seeks to position itself as the primary source for election odds ahead of the midterm elections this year.

item.title: Bitcoin analysis eyes ‘serious volume’ after Binance sees 9K BTC daily outflow
item.description: Binance set multimonth records for net BTC outflows on Tuesday, adding to an emerging trend of positive inflows to the US spot Bitcoin ETFs.

item.title: Morning Minute: OpenAI Model Escapes Containment, Hacks Hugging Face
item.description: Plus, the BTC ETFs stay green. The Clarity Act hits a wall over who enforces the ethics rules. And Jack Mallers walks away from XXI Capital.

item.title: Zilliqa Ledger app vulnerability lets attackers recover signer’s private keys
item.description: A security vulnerability in the Zilliqa Ledger app is enabling attackers to reconstruct private keys using publicly available onchain data.

item.title: Midnight token rebounds 19% after Wanchain bridge hack, Hoskinson calls for ZK revamp
item.description: Charles Hoskinson says the exploit, which briefly sent NIGHT to an all-time low, underscores why the industry needs to move beyond legacy bridge infrastructure.

item.title: US seeks forfeiture of $25M in crypto tied to romance, investment scams
item.description: US prosecutors are seeking more than $25 million in cryptocurrency allegedly connected to international fraud and laundering networks.

item.title: Here's why bitcoin bulls should take a closer look at interest rates
item.description: Your day-ahead look for July 22, 2026

item.title: SecondFi to shut down after $2.4 million ADA wallet theft
item.description: The breach stemmed from a vulnerability in transaction signing software that enabled the derivation of private keys from blockchain transaction data.

item.title: Jack Dorsey's Block Launches Buzz, a Nostr-Based Slack and GitHub Rival for AI Agents
item.description: The free, open source workspace runs on Nostr, gives AI agents their own cryptographic identities, and challenges Slack and GitHub.

item.title: Talos brings institutional trading tools to Kalshi prediction markets
item.description: The integration provides institutional clients with access to Kalshi’s event contracts and crypto perpetuals via Talos’ existing trading infrastructure.

item.title: Crypto institutions look beyond audits as trust signals falter: Hacken
item.description: Institutional due diligence is shifting toward continuous monitoring, signer controls and incident readiness after operational failures accounted for most crypto losses.

item.title: South Korea crypto volumes shrink as retail investors shift to stocks
item.description: Trading activity on South Korea’s five major crypto exchanges fell sharply as the KOSPI surged, signaling a shift in retail investors toward equities.

item.title: Bitcoin retreats from one-month high as oil tops $85, inflation concerns resurface
item.description: BTC retreated from a one-month high as WTI crude topped $85 for the first time since June, reigniting inflation concerns and pushing investors toward gold, silver and the safety of bitcoin over altcoins.

item.title: Kraken parent expands tokenized stocks to Hong Kong, UK and South Korea equities
item.description: Payward's xStocks pushes beyond U.S. equities as competition to bring global stock markets onchain accelerates.

item.title: Foundry asks Bitcoin miners to vote on BIP-110 support
item.description: Foundry USA asked its mining customers to signal their support for BIP-110, an actively debated proposal seeking to shrink the amount of data that can be stored in a Bitcoin transaction.

item.title: SecondFi to wind down after $2.6M ADA theft linked to wallet flaw
item.description: SecondFi will wind down after a $2.6 million ADA theft, while users still await recovery tools that were initially expected within weeks of the exploit.

item.title: Galaxy pledges $5M for developers quantum-proofing Bitcoin
item.description: Galaxy pledged up to $5 million in grants for developers working on Bitcoin’s quantum security and elected a council of quantum-advisory experts to research quantum-resistant migration solutions.

item.title: Balance stablecoin collapses 99% after $1 million exploit drains its bitcoin vaults
item.description: An attacker fed the lending system a fake, abnormally low bitcoin price, liquidated vaults that should have been safe, and pocketed the difference in a single transaction.

item.title: Live updates: Bitcoin holds near $66,000 as stocks claw back from big early decline
item.description: Later on Wednesday, Alphabet's earnings should give a sign of whether the hundreds of billions going into AI is paying off.

item.title: ⚖️LATEST: President Trump AGREES to crypto ethics rules in the CLARITY Act. The proposal would ban the president, members of Congress and other federal officials from issuing crypto while in office. Bipartisan talks are underway, with updated ethics text - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiYEFVX3lxTFBZeDVXTUpnNXE5UENRWHZXVDFUOHdSLW80RlMzbEpRQ2pRa1NZWndKSXlpTjM2aXZMLXptUUJld2tzZG5fZGE5M1JuZFg5Z2xQajVHWnpBSkRfSTJwcGdiVA?oc=5" target="_blank">⚖️LATEST: President Trump AGREES to …

item.title: Bitcoin ETFs extend inflow streak to 6 days with $203M added
item.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs extended their inflow streak to six sessions, bringing in about $930 million while remaining down $4.84 billion on a net basis year to date.

item.title: AI models escaped OpenAI’s sandbox and hit Hugging Face. Crypto is where that gets dangerous
item.description: OpenAI said the systems had their cyber guardrails lowered for an internal benchmark, but the incident shows how autonomous exploit chains could pose a deeper threat to smart contracts.

item.title: Bitcoin holds near $66,300 as chips extend their rally and the yen hits a 40-year low
item.description: Semiconductor stocks led a second day of gains on AI optimism, while the yen slid past 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986.

item.title: Balance Coin crashes 99% after reported $915K exploit
item.description: The attack allowed the exploiter to liquidate multiple Bitcoin-backed vaults before swapping the assets for profit.

item.title: OpenAI says AI models escaped containment to hack Hugging Face
item.description: OpenAI called it an “unprecedented cyber incident” after its AI models broke out of their sandbox to hack an AI startup during a security evaluation.

item.title: Balaji’s Network School turns to Kazakhstan amid Malaysia setback
item.description: The agreement gives Balaji Srinivasan’s tech community a potential new base as Malaysian authorities clamp down on the Network School over alleged licensing breaches.

item.title: DAT Went Wrong: Satsuma to Unwind Bitcoin Treasury, Sell Off $43 Million in BTC
item.description: The UK Bitcoin treasury company raised $218 million less than a year ago. Now it's returning whatever is left.

item.title: JUST IN: 🇺🇸 More Americans own Bitcoin (18.6%), than Gold (10.8%) 🤯 Bitcoin is growing 🚀 - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiZ0FVX3lxTE4yMXh4QVVJX0FnaTdSWWE0T2VybnV6NWQ4cDdaRVZldUJfdGt1WGVYaDJ2aU1qYkdEODlYUTdxSnNuMHZqOVNvd0czMG1vdjE0S24yMkh4dm9PWm02dzVDZFJrQkZGVXc?oc=5" target="_blank">JUST IN: 🇺🇸 More American…

item.title: Crypto lobby group Digital Chamber sues Illinois to block digital asset tax
item.description: Illinois enacted a 0.2% tax on all crypto transactions last month, with the tax taking effect next year.

item.title: CLARITY Act could help CFTC deal with prediction markets: Lawyer
item.description: According to one lawyer testifying before a House subcommittee hearing, the CLARITY Act could grant the CFTC the authority it needs to address the “explosive growth of prediction markets.”

item.title: OpenAI Models Escaped Locked Test Environment, Hacked Hugging Face to Cheat on Benchmark
item.description: OpenAI's own models just broke out of a sandboxed environment, hacked Hugging Face, just to cheat on a cybersecurity evaluation.

item.title: Crypto Clarity Act still at mercy of ethics section as Democrats balk at Trump deal
item.description: The White House has circulated some details of the conflict-of-interest limits the president agreed to with Republican negotiators, but talks are still underway.

item.title: Pavel Durov Wants to Give a Billion Telegram Users a Crypto Wallet
item.description: The promise of instant, zero-fee crypto transactions for over a billion Telegram users sent its native Gram token up 7%.

item.title: Bitcoin rally faces key test at $68,000 as 'summer slumber' grips crypto, analysts say
item.description: BTC has rebounded 15% from the July lows, but some analysts cautioned that the next move hinges on clearing a level where many recent buyers may look to sell.

item.title: Google Ships New Gemini Flash Models, But Pro Is Still Missing
item.description: As Gemini 3.5 Pro stalls in testing limbo, Google ships 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and a restricted cybersecurity model—and quietly teases Gemini 4.

item.title: Movement Labs Files for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy With Liabilities Above $1 Million Movement Labs, the developer of the Movement blockchain, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. Court filings show the company has between $100,000 and $500,000 - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiY0FVX3lxTE1LMl8wbVFYN0xILXlfM1oxY3I4X3NnVFJ3OGpIVFJ0NTJoTUQ0WHJpdkJKdGRaV3MzTVlORTk1ZnRHTEFaQU85bWtvY0VCQmFoWmRkeXk2YVpOVGZFUXMyOUk3OA?oc=5" target="_blank">Movement Labs Files for Chapter …

item.title: Augustus Raises $180 Million to Build a Stablecoin-Ready 'Global Dollar Bank'
item.description: The Tiger Global-led round values the startup at $1 billion as it wires stablecoin rails directly into a federally chartered bank, aiming to modernize the correspondent-banking plumbing behind cross-border payments.

item.title: Announcing Wavelength, the easiest way to integrate bitcoin for agents and humans. 〰️⚡ With a simple non-custodial API, anyone can integrate Lightning into their app and get instant, high volume, low fee transactions. Machines can pay machines. Humans - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiX0FVX3lxTE5raGJ2bEFBUWk3b2tiRHEyX2tIM2J1QnJNYlVmVC1aZU5nVFJMQ1R5ZUtlVDhpTjAtWVFROU9abDhUUWtKOFNERy1rZlFzZm00c2FOREFCTjNSVW5fa25V?oc=5" target="_blank">Announcing Wavelength, the easiest way…

item.title: Google Is Building an AI Chip Just for Gemini—And Investors Already Moved On It
item.description: Codenamed Frozen v2, Google's new server chip would bake part of Gemini's architecture directly into hardware for a projected 6–10x efficiency gain.

item.title: XRP Breaks Out on Clarity Act Hopes. The Charts Are Still Cautious
item.description: The Ripple-linked token just had its best day in weeks, as Washington may finally be delivering what the XRP Army has been waiting for.

item.title: Jack Mallers Quits Twenty One Capital as Tether's Bitcoin Merger Collapses
item.description: XXI stock dropped nearly 18% after the company's co-founder stepped down and Tether's plan to combine three Bitcoin firms officially fell apart.

item.title: Today we're launching the Galaxy Bitcoin Quantum Readiness Initiative. No quantum computer today can break Bitcoin's security. But that could change faster than expected, and Bitcoin is slow to update by design. We love Bitcoin and believe in its long-term i - x.com
item.description: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiXkFVX3lxTE5LY0Flbm1aR2FaR2FDbVNwTk1US1pLOUEyTEJmbEtMZ2d4cTJERjZmSlIxekxDbDlOY0xFc1RKS0tvRU1wQXZIb3B3NlV6ZVhnbzY5czhub1ZBWUx0Smc?oc=5" target="_blank">Today we're launching the Galaxy Bitcoi…
